title: Shakshuka (Eggs Poached in Spiced Tomato Sauce)

subs.name: 400 ml passata or one 400 g can crushed tomatoes
1 medium carrot or zucchini, diced
subs.notes: adds different texture but works well

subs.name: half the quantity of garam masala
subs.notes: changes flavour profile slightly but still delicious

steps.text: Heat oil in a wide, heavy-bottomed frying pan or kadai on medium flame. Add the onion and cook, stirring, for 5–6 minutes until soft and lightly golden.
Add the garlic and diced capsicum. Stir-fry for 3 minutes until the capsicum softens slightly and the garlic is fragrant but not brown.
Add the red chilli powder, cumin, coriander powder and turmeric. Stir for 30 seconds on a lower flame to bloom the spices without burning them.

Tip in the chopped tomatoes with salt and turn the flame to medium-high. Cook, breaking the tomatoes with the back of a spoon, for 8–10 minutes until the sauce thickens and oil begins to separate at the edges.

Use a spoon to make 5 small wells in the sauce. Crack an egg into each well. Cover the pan with a lid and cook on low flame for 5–7 minutes — whites should be just set while the yolks remain a little runny.
Scatter fresh coriander over the top and serve immediately straight from the pan, with bread or pita for scooping.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: capsicum → 1 medium carrot or zucchini, diced (adds different texture but works well); tomato → 400 ml passata or one 400 g can crushed tomatoes (skip chopping and add directly); cumin → half the quantity of garam masala (changes flavour profile slightly but still delicious)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Cook entirely in a kadai on a gas hob — no oven needed. Tomatoes and capsicum are available at every Indian market. Use desi ghee instead of olive oil for a richer taste. Serve with toasted bread, pav or paratha.
